title: myroxylon fragrans
intro: Myroxylon fragrans, also known as the Palo santo tree, is a tropical evergreen tree native to Latin America, particularly in the countries of Mexico, Costa Rica, and Ecuador. The tree's resin, obtained from the tree's trunk through a process of incision, has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Myroxylon fragrans include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, improve respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Myroxylon fragrans include essential oils, which can be used in aromatherapy, and the resin can be burned as a natural incense for its medicinal and spiritual benefits.
